properties.title: Kate Moss indulges her love of vintage cars as she is spotted zipping around London in classic British convertible
properties.summary: It is not clear whether she has bought the £120,000 car to add to her collection of classic vehicles, but she also posed behind the wheel of a RBW Roadster for an editorial photoshoot in last month's edition of EE72, the glossy magazine founded by former Vogue UK editor Edward Enninful. Moss is known for her love of vintage cars. Kate Moss was spotted driving around London in a white MGB convertible last week

properties.title: Countries gather in Colombia for the first international conference on how to phase out fossil fuels
properties.summary: Climate Conference. And this conference, it's the students who are very serious about acing the project, getting an A, more than 50 countries who want to find a way forward to tackle the main driver of climate change, which is fossil fuels. That's NPR's Julia Simon in Colombia.

properties.title: New front in the small-boat crisis as hundreds of migrants pour into Britain from Belgium following French clamp down
properties.summary: The migrants are using free buses that shuttle them along the coast to move from the so-called Jungle refugee camps in France to Belgium, where they are then picked up to cross the Channel. In an exclusive interview with The Mail on Sunday, Carl Decaluwe, governor of West Flanders, said he had never seen this many boats being launched from the Belgian beaches. It's estimated the distance by boat from De Panne to Dover is around 56 miles, compared to the over-20-mile crossing from Calais.

properties.title: Gaza holds first elections in 20 years amid stalled ceasefire process – but only in one city
properties.summary: Deir al-Balah, Gaza/Jerusalem (CNN) — Gaza held its first elections in more than 20 years on Saturday – but only in one city for a small fraction of the shattered territory’s population – amid a stalled process to advance the US-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Hamas. “Honestly, as a Palestinian and a son of the Gaza Strip, I feel proud that after this war, the democratic process is returning,” Mamdouh al-Bhaisi, a 52-year-old voter, told Reuters. Many voters viewed the election as a chance to express their desire for Palestinian self-determination and a way forward, especially after Israel’s bombardment of Gaza destroyed much of the territory.

properties.title: Taiwan foreign minister arrives in Eswatini after President Lai’s trip was blocked
properties.summary: Taiwan's Foreign Minister Lin Chia-lung said his delegation “overcame all obstacles” to get to Eswatini. - Taiwan’s Foreign Minister Lin Chia-lung arrived in Eswatini saying Taipei would not be held back by “authoritarian forces” after the Taiwanese government accused China of pressuring three African states to block overflight permission for President Lai Ching-te. Mr Lin did not offer details on his trip, saying only that his delegation “overcame all obstacles” to get to Eswatini in his capacity as Mr Lai’s special envoy to attend celebrations for the 40th anniversary of the accession of King Mswati III.
